summary: Large menu decompilation PR centered on `mnSound`, adding a substantial C implementation for the Sound options menu and related Sound Test work while cleaning up shared `mn` menu patterns. The PR introduced shared menu inlines, a `Menu` user-data struct, typed menu globals/prototypes, `Vec3` replacements for ad-hoc float triplets, and better `sislib` text typing. It also updated GALE01 symbol metadata for `mnSound`/`mnSoundTest` data, BSS, and string objects. Review mainly focused on Doxygen/comment style, casual naming for static helpers, and correcting a callback type in the menu GObj mirror struct.
what_changed: Added a 292-line implementation of `src/melee/mn/mnsound.c`, covering Sound menu setup, input handling, channel animation, volume slider animation, archive section loading, GObj/user-data initialization, and menu SFX behavior.
Added initial `src/melee/mn/mnsoundtest.c` work with Sound Test data arrays, externs, user-data shape, and function bodies, alongside a small `mnsoundtest.h` update.

Created `src/melee/mn/inlines.h` with shared menu helpers: `MenuEvent` bit flags, `GET_MENU`, animation-timer decrement, controller event retrieval, menu SFX wrappers, center-text initialization, and candidate animation helper inlines.
Added `Menu` and an `M2CTX` `Menu_GObj` mirror to `src/melee/mn/types.h`, replacing local anonymous menu user-data structs in `mndeflicker.c` and `mnhyaku.c`.
Expanded `mn_2295.h` from a single placeholder prototype to typed prototypes, `mn_unk1`/`mn_unk2` globals, and includes for `Vec3`, `HSD_TypeMask`, and `HSD_Archive`-related types.

Refactored `mndeflicker.c` and `mnhyaku.c` to use shared `Menu` access, `Menu_DecrementAnimTimer`, `Menu_GetEvents`, `Menu_SFX_*`, and `Vec3` instead of local duplicate extern structs and float-triplet placeholders.
Updated `sislib.h`/`sislib.c` to promote the formerly unknown text allocation return type to `HSD_Text`, including fields for global position, scale, and text-related state.
Added `lb_80011E24(HSD_JObj*, HSD_JObj**, ...)` to `lb_00F9.h` and noted its varargs child-JObj traversal behavior in `lb_00F9.c`.
Updated `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` to split and reclassify `mnSound` and `mnSoundTest` data objects, including float arrays, strings, BSS objects, and sdata strings.
smart_moves: Pulled recurring menu idioms into `mn/inlines.h`, reducing duplicate timer/event/SFX/text setup logic across `mndeflicker`, `mnhyaku`, and the new `mnsound` work.
Replaced ad-hoc `gobj_user_data` structs with a shared `Menu` type, making common menu fields such as `cursor` and `text` explicit and reusable.
Used `Vec3` for animation/translation triplets, improving type intent over local `struct ThreeFloats` or raw float arrays where the data is used as positions/ranges.
Split symbol-table data objects for `mnSound` into more precise float and string records, which makes future data matching and relocation reasoning more searchable.
Captured repeated menu audio calls behind small wrappers (`Menu_SFX_Back`, `Menu_SFX_Forward`, `Menu_SFX_Move`), making input-handler code read closer to behavior.
Added typed prototypes for shared `mn_2295` functions used by several menu files, reducing local extern drift.
decomp_lessons: When several menu files independently recover the same user-data layout, promote it into a shared `types.h` struct before continuing decompilation; this avoids repeated anonymous structs and makes field names converge.
For menu input handlers, naming event bit masks (`MenuEvent_Back`, `MenuEvent_Left`, etc.) can make branch behavior clear without changing matching-sensitive control flow.
Use small static inline wrappers for repeated sound/menu operations, but keep names concise; reviewers suggested static helpers do not need long exported-style names.

`lb_80011E24` appears to take a root `HSD_JObj*`, output pointers, and a variable list of child indices terminated by `-1`; documenting this varargs convention is valuable for future JObj traversal decompilation.
Float-order hacks can sometimes be removed once shared text initialization helpers preserve literal ordering and call structure acceptably.
Updating `symbols.txt` object sizes and data classifications is part of decomp progress, especially when contiguous unknown data resolves into float arrays and strings.
naming_conventions: Menu event enum values used `MenuEvent_Up`, `MenuEvent_Down`, `MenuEvent_Left`, `MenuEvent_Right`, `MenuEvent_Unk`, and `MenuEvent_Back` with bit-shift definitions.
Shared menu helpers were named with a `Menu_` prefix, e.g. `Menu_DecrementAnimTimer`, `Menu_GetEvents`, `Menu_SFX_Back`, and `Menu_InitCenterText`.
Reviewer feedback suggested static/private helper names can be more casual and less globally styled, e.g. `sfxForward(void)` instead of `Menu_SFX_Forward` for a static helper.
Recovered menu user-data fields began as generic `cursor`, `unk1`, `unk2`, `unk3`, and `text`; comments explicitly note uncertainty about whether the unknown bytes are arbitrary per-menu variables.
Typed structs for still-unknown globals were named `mn_unk1` and `mn_unk2`, preserving uncertainty rather than inventing semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(4)` in `mndeflicker.c` where stack shape still needed matching assistance.
Kept candidate inline helpers such as `inline_test_3` and `inline_test_4` with TODO notes that one may match deduplicated clones like `fn_8017435C`, `fn_80174338`, and `fn_80174380`.
Preserved matching-sensitive local temporaries and comments, including `f32 arg8 = 0.0F; // this should be uninitialized` in `mnsound.c`.
Used `lb_80011E24(..., index, -1)` sequences to recover nested `HSD_JObj` pointers before applying `HSD_JObjReqAnimAll`, `mn_8022F3D8`, `HSD_JObjAnimAll`, `HSD_JObjGetTranslation`, and `mn_8022ED6C`.
Used current animation frame helpers such as `mn_8022F298`/`mn_8022EC18` to preserve animation state while switching menu channel or volume visuals.
Moved repeated center-text allocation and setup into `Menu_InitCenterText`, keeping exact literals such as `-9.5F`, `9.1F`, `17.0F`, `364.68332F`, `38.38772F`, and `0.0521F` in one place.

review_feedback.feedback: Doxygen `@tag` comments should use `/** ... */` multiline comments or `///` single-line comments to be recognized by docs tooling.
Static functions can use more casual/private-style names; reviewer suggested something like `sfxForward(void)`.
Use Doxygen trailing comments such as `///< @returns a bitfield of controller inputs` so docs and `clangd` intellisense display the information.
Project style prefers longer doc comments in corresponding `.dox` files, e.g. `mn_2295.dox`, though brief header comments were acceptable for now.
Reviewer corrected `Menu_GObj.user_data_remove_func` to take `Menu* data` instead of `Fighter* data`.
Reviewer suggested converting inline `//` field comments on `HSD_Text` to Doxygen `///<` comments for generated documentation.
Reviewer noted that sourcing `tools/vimrc` in Vim/Neovim provides Doxygen highlighting.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `Menu` fields `unk1`, `unk2`, or `unk3` in `src/melee/mn/types.h`.
Find later PRs that complete or rename `mnSound_802492CC`, `mnSound_80249C08`, or `fn_80249A1C`.
Search for later cleanup of `mn/inlines.h` static helper names after reviewer suggestion to use casual private names.
Search for later `.dox` additions for `mn_2295` or Doxygen conversion of comments added in this PR.
Find later work that confirms the `lb_80011E24` varargs JObj traversal signature and semantics.
Search for later PRs that replace `soundtest_user_data` with shared `Menu` or a better Sound Test-specific struct.
